Mann–Whitney U - Exceedances & Permutations (SLLS vs. unconstrained)

Compiled at 2026-02-02 19:13:01 UTC

here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "395a0ba5-185a-4db3-a7c0-d661b80ab60f")

In this script we extend the exceedance study to iterate over the number of permutations B and the starting exceedances exceed0. We compare unconstrained vs. constrained (SLLS rule) GPD fits against the Mann–Whitney U reference.

Design

## # A tibble: 16 × 3
##    n_per_group     B exceed0
##          <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1         100  1000  100   
##  2         100  1000  250   
##  3         100  1000    0.25
##  4         100  1000    0.1 
##  5         100 10000  100   
##  6         100 10000  250   
##  7         100 10000    0.25
##  8         100 10000    0.1 
##  9         500  1000  100   
## 10         500  1000  250   
## 11         500  1000    0.25
## 12         500  1000    0.1 
## 13         500 10000  100   
## 14         500 10000  250   
## 15         500 10000    0.25
## 16         500 10000    0.1

Notes:

  • If exceed0 < 1, we treat it as a proportion of B (e.g., 0.25 → ceiling(0.25*B)); otherwise it’s used as a fixed integer.
  • We never trim the tail to fewer exceedances than requested if the observed statistic already sits in the tail.

Simulation (once per \(n, B\))

Helpers for filenames & exceed0 handling

permApprox runs

We compute both unconstrained and constrained (SLLS) \(p\)-values for every scenario. All results are saved per-scenario and then combined safely afterward.

Safe combination of results

## Columns: 14

How often are the approximated p-values smaller?

Counts and shares of approximated p-values smaller than Mann-Whitney U p-values.
n B_perm exc0 n_tests # smaller (unconstr) # smaller (constr) % smaller (unconstr) % smaller (constr)
100 1000 0.10 1000 673 384 67.3% 38.4%
100 1000 0.25 1000 750 206 75.0% 20.6%
100 1000 100.00 1000 673 384 67.3% 38.4%
100 1000 250.00 1000 750 206 75.0% 20.6%

100 10000 250.00 1000 647 543 64.7% 54.3%

500 10000 0.25 1000 905 104 90.5% 10.4%

500 10000 250.00 1000 765 313 76.5% 31.3%
